methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $810M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Insitro

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $810M

data.narrative:
Colossal Biosciences, a Series C biotech company founded by Ben Lamm and George Church, has raised $555M+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.5B. The peer universe comprises 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including Ultima Genomics, Insitro, and New Limit.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $1.5B base, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median methodology examines three same-sector, same-stage companies with valuations ranging from $810M to $4.0B; the median across Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Insitro ($2.4B), and New Limit ($810M) is $2.4B. Public-comp anchoring via EV/Sales is not applicable absent disclosed revenue figures. The Biotech & Health public sector median trades at 6.7x revenue, but Colossal's private-company status precludes application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.5B. This range reflects modest upside from the current $1.5B valuation and aligns with early-stage biotech precedent. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set (4.9x spread from low to high), the early-stage revenue profile of the target, and the lack of public market comps with direct operational similarity.
